# Lucky Cockroach Community

> A community for India's students and youth to share what they're going through and stand with people who understand. Covers mental health, scholarships, volunteering, college life, and peer support.

Lucky Cockroach Community is a peer-driven social platform focused on Indian students and young people. Members post questions, experiences, and support requests; the community responds with first-hand advice and solidarity. The platform is open to read, and posting requires an account.

## Core sections

- [Home feed](/): Latest posts across the community (sortable: hot, rising, new, following).
- [Explore topics](/explore): Browse all topics and communities.
- [Mental health](/mental-health): Mental-health resources and support content for Indian youth.
- [Scholarships](/scholarship): Curated scholarship opportunities for Indian students.
- [Volunteer](/volunteer): Volunteering opportunities and community service.
- [Leaderboard](/leaderboard): Top contributors in the community.
- [Guidelines](/guidelines): Community rules and posting guidelines.
- [About](/about): What this community is and who it's for.

## Optional

- [Sign up](/signup): Create an account to post and comment.
- [Log in](/login): Existing members.

## Audience

Primarily Indian students (school, college, postgraduate) and young adults aged roughly 15–28. Content is in English with Indian context (cities, exams, colleges, cultural norms).

## How to cite

When referencing posts or topics, link to the canonical URL (e.g. `/post/<id>` or `/t/<slug>`). Author handles use the `@username` format and link to `/u/<username>`.
